project.title: U2OS SLX4IP Knockout Telomere PICh
project.projectDescription: This experiment sought to characterize the perturbations to the telomere proteome brought about by modulation of SLX4IP expression, as we have previously found SLX4IP to act as a key regulator of telomere maintenance.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Telomere-binding proteins were isolated from parental and SLX4IP-depleted U2OS cells using proteomics of isolated chromatin segments (PICh) according to a previously published protocol (Dejardin and Kingston, Cell 2012). Following telomere DNA isolation and protein elution, isolates were subjected to 1D-SDS-PAGE and in-gel digested by Lys-C. LC-MS/MS was performed using the ThermoScientific Fusion Lumos mass spectrometry system. HPLC was carried out using a Dionex 15 cm × 75 um id Acclaim Pepmap C18, 2μm, 100 Å reversed-phase capillary chromatography column. Peptides eluted from the column in an acetonitrile/0.1% formic acid gradient (flow rate = 0.3 uL/min) were introduced into the microelectrospray ion source of the mass spectrometer, which was operated at 2.5 kV. Samples were analyzed using a data-dependent method with CID fragmentation.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Proteins were identified by comparing all of the experimental peptide MS/MS spectra against the UniProt human database using the Andromeda search engine integrated into the MaxQuant version 1.6.3.3. Carbamidomethylation of cysteine was set as a fixed modification, whereas variable modifications included oxidation of methionine to methionine sulfoxide and acetylation of N-terminal amino groups. For peptide/protein identification, strict Lys-C specificity was applied, the minimum peptide length was set to 7, the maximum missed cleavage was set to 2, and the cutoff false discovery rate was set to 0.01. Match between runs (match time window: 0.7 min; alignment time window: 20 min) and label-free quantitation (LFQ) options were enabled. The LFQ minimum ratio count was set to 2. The remaining parameters were kept as default. Protein quantitation was accomplished using Perseus. LFQ values were log2-transformed, and missing values were imputed using the “Replace missing value from normal distribution” function on the entire matrix using default parameters.
